note: seq=43 (PGDOOR, lifeline-grade, PG-SAFE): open the Hetzner Postgres to EXACTLY ONE scoped peer -- the InterServer worker node 64.20.50.3/32 -- for role 'atlas' over scram-sha-256, so the second node's enrich worker can connect and contribute rows beyond this box's ~1,200/min ceiling. HIGHEST blast-radius change in the project (it can require a PG restart + a firewall rule on the production DB), so it is built to self-heal and to satisfy the puller's own post-apply health gate (pg-accepting, pg-schema, sshd-listening, ufw-ssh-allowed). The work is done by /opt/atlas/pg_door_open.py (fetched + sha-pinned + --selftest gated). SAFETY: (1) backs up pg_hba.conf + postgresql.conf before any edit; (2) adds ONE scoped pg_hba host line (host <db> atlas 64.20.50.3/32 scram-sha-256) -- additive, idempotent, no existing rule touched; (3) applies pg_hba with `systemctl reload postgresql` (ZERO downtime); (4) only if listen_addresses is purely loopback does it set listen_addresses='*' and `systemctl restart postgresql`, GUARDED by a pg_isready readiness loop with AUTO-RESTORE -- if PG is not ready within 60s it restores the original config, restarts until PG is back UP, and fails loud (door not opened, PG alive); (5) UFW: re-asserts SSH (OpenSSH + 22/tcp) FIRST so the box can never be stranded, then adds a SCOPED `allow from 64.20.50.3 to any port 5432 proto tcp`; it NEVER enables ufw from inactive, NEVER disables ufw, NEVER removes the ssh rule (keeps the puller ufw-ssh-allowed check green); if ufw is inactive it reports that the cloud firewall governs 5432. NO systemctl stop/disable/mask anywhere -- only reload/restart. Idempotent: re-runs add nothing and reload-only. Allowlist-safe: every cmd starts with /opt/atlas/ (systemctl/ufw/sudo are invoked INSIDE the python helper, not as manifest cmds). HONEST: not run against live PG from the sandbox (no Postgres there); what IS verified -- py_compile clean and --selftest PASS (hba line build + present-detection incl. comment/whitespace/ip-mismatch cases, listen_addresses loopback-vs-exposed parser, ufw scoped-rule parser). The box run prints PGDOOR_RESULT={...} with hba_present, listen_addresses, ufw state, and a local pg_isready -h 168.119.226.254 probe as box-side proof the door is open; the operator then runs the InterServer one-shot to get the remote 5432 answer from 64.20.50.3. Lands AFTER seq-41/42 (additive batch) per strict in-order drain.

steps.note: seq=43 PGDOOR: scoped, self-healing PG door for InterServer 64.20.50.3/32. reload-first; restart only if loopback-only, with pg_isready auto-restore; scoped UFW; ssh kept allowed.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/pg_door_open.py':'3b04a87ae4e1854198d0c1a8b87a4731074cb51cb241f227fd7d3451ba044f0c'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"
